summary: Closed draft PR adding missing implementations for lb_80019628, lbMemory_80015320, and lbMemory_8001564C, plus a small fn_80015184 matching improvement and a public lbMemory_80015320 prototype. Bot report showed one new match for fn_80015184, three unmatched-item improvements, two broken matches, and two regressions.

key_files.role: Added missing lb_80019628 implementation that updates timing state, PAD sampling rate, and OS periodic alarm scheduling.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lb_80019628 improved 0.00% to 50.52%

key_files.role: Added includes for AR and devcom, moved lbmemory.c string and LBMgr struct earlier, implemented lbMemory_80015320 and lbMemory_8001564C, and changed fn_80015184 alarm reset from a raw tick constant/null handler to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) with fn_80015184.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSSetAlarm changed from 0x10624DD3, NULL to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), fn_80015184

key_files.role: Added public prototype for lbMemory_80015320 with recovered parameter types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added /* 015320 */ void lbMemory_80015320(s32, Handle*, void*, s32);

what_changed.text: Implemented lb_80019628 in lb_0192.c with timer-derived tick selection, sampling-rate clamping to 11, PADSetSamplingRate calls, alarm cancellation/recreation, and OSSetPeriodicAlarm using lb_80019900 as handler.

Implemented lbMemory_80015320 in lbmemory.c, including HSD_DevComRequest path for low addresses, an LBMgr alarm-backed transfer path for other addresses, recursive handling of next Handle entries, and final callback through g_alloc.x6E8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void lbMemory_80015320(s32 arg0, Handle* arg1, void* arg2, s32 cancelflag)

what_changed.text: Implemented lbMemory_8001564C to allocate AR arena space, initialize arena high/low fields, build two Handle freelists with for loops and NULL terminators, and initialize g_alloc.x69C via lbMemory_80014E24.
Changed fn_80015184 alarm rescheduling to use OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) and fn_80015184 instead of a raw constant and NULL callback.
Exposed lbMemory_80015320 in lbmemory.h, replacing the previous source-local static declaration with a header prototype.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed static declaration in lbmemory.c

smart_moves.text: Used project/Dolphin macros and APIs for timing and alarms, notably OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), OSSetAlarm, OSSetPeriodicAlarm, OSCreateAlarm, and OSCancelAlarm, rather than preserving only raw tick constants.
Used natural for loops to build repeated Handle freelist links in lbMemory_8001564C instead of writing repeated stride-0xC assignments by hand.
Moved lbMemory_80015320 from a source-local static forward declaration to the owning header after adding a body, aligning the declaration surface with the recovered function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed /* 015320 */ static void lbMemory_80015320(int, Handle*, int, int)
raw_diff: added /* 015320 */ void lbMemory_80015320(s32, Handle*, void*, s32)

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated handle initialization differs only by array index and stride, a natural counted for loop with a final NULL terminator can express the likely authored source and keep initialization reviewable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C for loops over g_alloc.x8 and g_alloc.x638

decomp_lessons.lesson: Replacing raw alarm tick constants with SDK timing macros can improve match/readability when the macro value corresponds to the observed period and the callback shape is supported by nearby code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSSetAlarm changed to OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), fn_80015184

decomp_lessons.lesson: A recovered function body should be paired with the real owning header prototype when the function is externally callable; avoid leaving stale static or unknown local declarations.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style names were retained for not-yet-semantic globals/functions such as lb_80019628, lb_804329F0, lbMemory_80015320, and lbl_803BA2C0; the PR does not provide evidence for semantic renames.

Recovered local struct name LBMgr and fields src, dst, size, offset, cb_arg, and cb express a transfer manager layout, but the PR evidence does not establish whether these are final project-wide canonical names.

Some generated/decompiler-style local names remain, including temp_r30, temp_r28, temp_r3, temp_r26, temp_r29, temp_r31, temp_r25, var_r0, and sp14, indicating naming cleanup was not complete in this slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used SDK macro OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) to replace raw constant 0x10624DD3 in fn_80015184; bot report shows fn_80015184 became a new 100% match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report fn_80015184 +32 bytes, 88.61% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used explicit pointer casts into g_alloc storage for Handle freelists and LBMgr placement, including ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0), likely as a matching bridge while the allocator layout remains partially byte-array based.
Kept assert calls as direct __assert with lbmemory.c string-derived message pointers in lbMemory_80015320 rather than converting them to HSD_ASSERT-style macros in this slice.
Used HSD_DevComRequest and AR library calls, requiring new includes <baselib/devcom.h> and <dolphin/ar.h>.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/lb/lbmemory fn_80015184 improved from 88.61% to 100.00%.
Bot reported three improvements in unmatched items: lbMemory_8001564C 0.00% to 93.52%, lbMemory_80015320 0.00% to 91.00%, and lb_80019628 0.00% to 50.52%.
Bot reported two broken matches in lbmemory: lbMemory_80014E24 100.00% to 99.22% and lbMemory_800154D4 100.00% to 99.25%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment decomp-dev[bot] broken matches table

review_feedback.text: Bot reported regressions in unmatched items: lbmemory .data 92.18% to 90.32% and lbMemory_80014FC8 99.32% to 99.00%.
No human review comments or review approvals are present in the loaded slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural counted loops for Handle freelist initialization
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbmemory-style freelist setup, repeated stride-based Handle link initialization can be expressed as counted for loops with a final NULL terminator when the only variation is index/stride.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C for loops over g_alloc.x8 and g_alloc.x638

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer SDK time macros over raw alarm constants when evidence matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: For OS alarm scheduling, replacing a raw tick constant with OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3) plus the observed callback improved fn_80015184 to a bot-reported 100% match in this PR.

source_update_candidates.title: lbmemory.c contains AR-backed allocator and async transfer work in this PR slice
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2490 adds lbMemory_80015320 and lbMemory_8001564C in src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c, using ARAlloc/ARFree/ARGetSize, HSD_DevComRequest, OSAlarm, and Handle freelist initialization.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include additions for dolphin/ar.h and baselib/devcom.h
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful source-owned path fact candidate, but the PR was closed unmerged and includes regressions, so curator should verify against current source before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_0192.c owns lb_80019628 timing/sampling update candidate
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2490 adds lb_80019628 to src/melee/lb/lb_0192.c; the function updates lb_804329F0 timing fields, clamps PAD sampling rate, and schedules a periodic OS alarm with lb_80019900.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful lookup/path candidate for future lb_0192 work, but implementation was only a partial match and the PR was not merged in loaded metadata.

source_update_candidates.title: Assert macro cleanup follow-up for generated __assert blocks
source_update_candidates.text: This PR leaves direct __assert calls in newly added lbMemory_80015320 using lbmemory.c string-derived message pointers. Curator may want to cross-check whether this is an exception or a missed HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG cleanup case.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards/example follow-up only; PR evidence does not prove whether macro conversion would match.

rejection_notes: Do not promote lbMemory_80015320, lbMemory_8001564C, or lb_80019628 as accepted final matches: bot report shows only partial improvements for those three functions.
Do not infer that the PR was merged or accepted; loaded metadata shows CLOSED with merged_at null and draft true.
Do not infer human reviewer feedback; no review comments or reviews are present.
Do not treat the LBMgr field names as globally canonical without current-source or symbol evidence.
Do not treat direct __assert usage as an accepted style from this PR alone, because loaded standards prefer assert/report macros when evidence supports them.
